what_it_does: Renders a friendly, operator-oriented HTML form from a tool's JSON Schema and an enriched ToolDescriptor, with optional operation-conditional parameter visibility and human labels.
observations: The component is generic: it consumes any ToolDescriptor and renders controls for each property in tool.inputSchema.properties.
It supports an op-conditional mode: when tool.opField is set, it renders a discriminator selector first and then only the params listed in tool.opParams for the chosen op.
Labels and help text are resolved from authored tool.labels, then schema title/description, then the raw param name.
Enums with more than four options render as a select dropdown; four or fewer render as a pill button group.
Enum option labels can be authored via tool.enumLabels; empty string options are labeled 'Any'.
Boolean properties render as a toggle switch; numeric properties use type=number; param names matching a multiline regex render as textarea; everything else renders as a text input.
Requiredness comes from inputSchema.required or from op-conditional tool.opRequired[op].
The component uses controlled inputs and includes aria attributes such as aria-pressed, aria-checked, aria-labelledby, and role=group/switch.
standouts: Operation-conditional parameter visibility driven by tool.opParams.
Friendly label resolution that never exposes raw param names or enum values by default.
Adaptive enum control choice based on a hard-coded threshold of four options.
conventions: TypeScript React functional component with file-local helper functions.
CSS class namespace prefix 'tf-' for form elements.
Controlled form inputs with onChange callbacks returning values to a parent.
Accessibility attributes on custom pill and toggle controls.
concerns: No support for array or object schema properties; only scalar types are handled.
Assumes enum values are strings; non-string enum values may not render or compare correctly.
Number inputs return an empty string when cleared, which may conflict with a schema expecting a number.
The multiline heuristic is name-based and may misclassify fields.
No validation feedback, error messages, or schema constraint enforcement is visible.
notes: A schema-driven form renderer that tries to hide implementation details from operators through op-conditional layout and human labels.
questions: Where are ToolDescriptor extensions such as opField, opParams, opLabels, opRequired, labels, and enumLabels defined and populated?
How are the 'tf-*' CSS classes styled, and is there a design system contract?
How does the parent component handle submission, validation, and conversion of empty numeric values?
Are there plans to support array/object schemas, references, or conditional JSON Schema keywords?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it builds a friendly, op-conditional form from a JSON schema and enriched tool metadata.
apparent_themes: Operator ergonomics over raw schema fidelity
intention_signals: It appears designed to let a single generic form component serve many tools by adapting layout, labels, and visible fields based on an operation discriminator, so operators see only relevant, friendly controls.
novelty: The combination of op-conditional parameter ordering/visibility, authored per-operation labels, and an enum-size threshold for control choice is a distinct UX pattern for schema-generated forms.
contribution: A reusable, schema-driven form renderer that keeps tool-specific labeling and operation-conditional field visibility out of the component itself, allowing a single component to serve many tools.
summary_for_embedding: SchemaForm is a React component that renders a friendly, accessible, operation-conditional form from a tool descriptor and JSON schema, choosing controls based on property type and enum size and resolving human labels from authored metadata.
intention_for_embedding: Intended to provide a single generic form UI for many tools by hiding raw schema names and irrelevant parameters behind an operation selector and human labels.

symbols.does: Resolves a human-readable label and optional help text for a parameter.
symbols.description: Prefers authored labels from tool.labels[name], falls back to the JSON Schema property title, and uses the raw name as a last resort; help comes from the schema description.

symbols.does: Determines which parameter names should be shown for the current operation.
symbols.description: When an opField and opParams are configured, returns the authored parameter list for the selected op while excluding the op selector itself; otherwise returns all non-op parameters.

symbols.does: Checks whether a parameter is required for the current form state.
symbols.description: Returns true if the parameter is listed in the schema's required array or in the op-conditional required list for the selected operation.

symbols.does: Renders the complete form for a tool descriptor.
symbols.description: Exported React component that computes the current operation, renders the op selector first if present, and then maps visible parameters to friendly controls using renderField.

symbols.does: Renders a single parameter control with label, help, and required marker.
symbols.description: Chooses between a select dropdown, pill group, toggle switch, number input, textarea, or text input based on the property's enum, type, and name heuristic, then wraps it in a labeled field.

symbols.does: Resolves a friendly display label for an enum option.
symbols.description: Looks up tool.enumLabels[name][opt] and falls back to 'Any' for an empty string option or the raw option value otherwise.
